# TrustScope — full text

> Concatenated page text for engines that compose longer answers without crawling each
> URL. Canonical source is the live site; this file is a convenience mirror.

## TrustScope (https://trustscope.neckarshore.ai)

Don't build on code you haven't vetted. TrustScope reads a public GitHub repository and
returns a deterministic three-pillar trust report — Security & Supply Chain, Trust &
Governance, and Community & Sustainability — with constructive, upstream-friendly fixes
and no misleading single score. It is built on the OpenSSF Scorecard, made in Germany by
Neckarshore AI. Free to read, anonymous, no sign-in required.

The three pillars each answer a different question:
- Security & Supply Chain — "Is it built securely?" The full OpenSSF Scorecard: token
  permissions, pinned dependencies, SAST, signed releases, branch protection, and more.
- Trust & Governance — "Can I trust the project behind it?" License, security policy, who
  owns it, and whether there is a way to reach someone when something breaks.
- Community & Sustainability — "Will it be here in a year?" Maintenance, contributors, and
  recent activity — read as a lifecycle stage, never as a grade.

## How it works (https://trustscope.neckarshore.ai/how-it-works)

TrustScope turns a public GitHub repo into a deterministic three-pillar trust report with
constructive, upstream-friendly fixes and no misleading single score. Four steps:
1. Paste a repo — any public GitHub repository, as a URL or owner/repo.
2. Assess it with OpenSSF — the full OpenSSF Scorecard, plus GitHub governance and
   lifecycle signals.
3. Read your report by pillar — each pillar answers a different question, with its own
   findings and constructive fixes. No single grade papers over the trade-offs: you see
   where the project is strong and where it isn't.
4. Send fixes upstream — file a friendly, attributed issue as yourself.

Why no single score? Each pillar answers a different question. A brilliant, secure library
maintained by one person is not "7 out of 10" — it is strong on security and early on
community. Collapsing that into one number hides exactly the trade-off you are trying to
weigh, so TrustScope keeps the pillars separate.

## FAQ (https://trustscope.neckarshore.ai/faq)

What is TrustScope? TrustScope reads a public GitHub repository across three pillars —
Security & Supply Chain, Trust & Governance, and Community & Sustainability — and gives a
verdict per pillar, with no misleading single score.

What is an adopter, and what is a maintainer? An adopter is evaluating someone else's
project before depending on it; a maintainer runs their own project to see and close the
trust gaps adopters look for. Both read the same three-pillar report from opposite
directions.

Do I need an account to read a report? No. Reading any report is anonymous and needs no
sign-in. Accounts (to save history and email reports) are coming later.

Why is there no single score? Each pillar answers a different question. Collapsing
security, governance and community into one number hides the exact trade-off you are
trying to weigh — so TrustScope keeps them separate.

Where does the data come from? The full OpenSSF Scorecard plus public GitHub governance
and lifecycle signals. The same repository always produces the same report — it is
deterministic, with no LLM in the loop.

Does TrustScope store my data? Reading a report stores nothing about you. It is
self-hosted, GDPR-clean, with no third-party trackers.

## For adopters (https://trustscope.neckarshore.ai/for/adopters)

Vet a third-party open-source project before you depend on it — security, governance and
community, with the trade-offs kept visible. The xz backdoor showed how supply-chain risk
stays invisible until it detonates; TrustScope surfaces the signals a project already
emits before you pull it into your build.

## For maintainers (https://trustscope.neckarshore.ai/for/maintainers)

See — and close — the trust gaps adopters look for in your own project, with constructive
rule-based fixes you can file yourself.

## About (https://trustscope.neckarshore.ai/about)

TrustScope is an open-source trust report by Neckarshore AI — a studio in Stuttgart,
Germany. It runs on its own standards: Neckarshore assesses its own repositories with the
same report it hands to everyone else. TrustScope grew out of a Product Trust & Quality
Framework — turning the public signals a project already emits into a calm, three-pillar
read on how much you can trust it, and never faking the fourth (Functional Quality), the
one only a human can judge. Made in Germany: self-hosted fonts, no third-party trackers,
and a privacy posture that matches EU expectations by default. Open: TrustScope builds on
the public OpenSSF Scorecard and is deterministic — the same repository always produces
the same report.
